rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type contracts in the component type scope and implementation-level bindings in the implementation scope
Use valid component type scope for assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports
Preserve subcomponent ports in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure that assumptions and guarantees are placed in valid scopes
Use valid feature names and visible identifiers in AGREE contracts
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assume` and `guarantee` for assumptions and guarantees
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `const` for constant declarations
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
